briefdescription.para: Callback function.

detaileddescription.para: The first argument is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.

briefdescription.para: This function is the handler which performs processing to send data to the TMR_Manager.

detaileddescription.para: It is called from an interrupt context such that the amount of processing performed should be minimized. It is called when the transmit FIFO of the TMR_Manager is empty and more data can be sent through the TMR_Manager.
This handler provides an example of how to handle data for the TMR_Manager, but is application specific.

parameterdescription.para: contains a callback reference from the driver. In this case it is the instance pointer for the TMR_Manager driver.

memberdef.name: XTMR_Manager_ConfigTable
memberdef.initializer:
= { { XPAR_TMRMANAGER_0_DEVICE_ID, XPAR_TMRMANAGER_0_BASEADDR, XPAR_TMRMANAGER_0_BRK_DELAY_RST_VALUE, XPAR_TMRMANAGER_0_MASK_RST_VALUE, XPAR_TMRMANAGER_0_MAGIC1, XPAR_TMRMANAGER_0_MAGIC2, XPAR_TMRMANAGER_0_UE_IS_FATAL, XPAR_TMRMANAGER_0_UE_WIDTH, XPAR_TMRMANAGER_0_NO_OF_COMPARATORS, XPAR_TMRMANAGER_0_COMPARATORS_MASK, XPAR_TMRMANAGER_0_WATCHDOG, XPAR_TMRMANAGER_0_WATCHDOG_WIDTH, XPAR_TMRMANAGER_0_SEM_INTERFACE, XPAR_TMRMANAGER_0_SEM_HEARTBEAT_WATCHDOG, XPAR_TMRMANAGER_0_SEM_HEARTBEAT_WATCHDOG_WIDTH,
XPAR_TMRMANAGER_0_BRK_DELAY_WIDTH, XPAR_TMRMANAGER_0_TMR, XPAR_TMRMANAGER_0_TEST_COMPARATOR, XPAR_TMRMANAGER_0_STRICT_MISCOMPARE, XPAR_TMRMANAGER_0_USE_DEBUG_DISABLE, XPAR_TMRMANAGER_0_USE_TMR_DISABLE }, }

briefdescription.para: The configuration table for TMR Manager devices.
The configuration table for TMR Manager devices.
Initialize a XTMR_Manager instance.

detaileddescription.para: The receive and transmit FIFOs of the core are not flushed, so the user may want to flush them. The hardware device does not have any way to disable the receiver such that any valid data may be present in the receive FIFO. This function disables the core interrupt. The baudrate and format of the data are fixed in the hardware at hardware build time.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTMR_Manager instance.

is a reference to a structure containing information about a specific TMR Manager device. This function initializes an InstancePtr object for a specific device specified by the contents of Config. This function can initialize multiple instance objects with the use of multiple calls giving different Config information on each call.

is the device base address in the virtual memory address space. The caller is responsible for keeping the address mapping from EffectiveAddr to the device physical base address unchanged once this function is invoked. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping changes after this function is called. If address translation is not used, use Config->BaseAddress for this parameters, passing the physical address instead.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if everything starts up as expected.

simplesect.para: The Config pointer argument is not used by this function, but is provided to keep the function signature consistent with other drivers.

briefdescription.para: Initialize a XTMR_Manager instance.

detaileddescription.para: The receive and transmit FIFOs of the core are not flushed, so the user may want to flush them. The hardware device does not have any way to disable the receiver such that any valid data may be present in the receive FIFO. This function disables the core interrupt. The baudrate and format of the data are fixed in the hardware at hardware build time.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTMR_Manager instance.

is the unique id of the device controlled by this XTMR_Manager instance. Passing in a device id associates the generic XTMR_Manager instance to a specific device, as chosen by the caller or application developer.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if everything starts up as expected.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if the device is not found in the configuration table.

briefdescription.para: Set the user recovery handler, which can replace the pre-defined handler completely, to do custom recovery.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTMR_Manager instance.

simplesect.para: This function is run in interrupt context.

briefdescription.para: Set the user pre-reset handler, which can be used to save context before reset in a recovery sequence.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTMR_Manager instance.

simplesect.para: This function is run in interrupt context.

briefdescription.para: Set the user post-reset handler, which can be used to restore context after reset in a recovery sequence.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTMR_Manager instance.

briefdescription.para: Returns a snapshot of the current statistics in the structure specified.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTMR_Manager instance.
is a pointer to a XTMR_ManagerStats structure to where the statistics are to be copied.

briefdescription.para: This function zeros the statistics for the given instance.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTMR_Manager instance.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the core interrupt such that an interrupt will occur when any of the SEM status signals indicated by the mask are changed.

detaileddescription.para: This function enables the core interrupt such that an interrupt will occur when an unmasked SEM status input changes value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTMR_Manager instance.
is a mask indicating bits that should give an interrupt.
is a pointer to the XTMR_Manager instance.

briefdescription.para: This function disables the core interrupt.

detaileddescription.para: After calling this function, data may still be received by the core but no interrupt will be generated since the hardware device has no way to disable the receiver.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTMR_Manager instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the handler that will be called when an event (interrupt) occurs in the driver.

detaileddescription.para: The purpose of the handler is to allow application specific processing to be performed.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XTMR_Manager instance.
FuncPtr
is the pointer to the callback function.
CallBackRef
is the upper layer callback reference passed back when the callback function is invoked.
None.
There is no assert on the CallBackRef since the driver doesn't know what it is (nor should it) The purpose of the handler is to allow application specific processing to be performed.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTMR_Manager instance .
is the pointer to the callback function.
is the upper layer callback reference passed back when the callback function is invoked.

simplesect.para: There is no assert on the CallBackRef since the driver doesn't know what it is (nor should it)

briefdescription.para: Runs a self-test on the device hardware.

detaileddescription.para: Since there is no way to perform a loopback in the hardware, this test can only check the state of the status register to verify it is correct. This test assumes that the hardware device is still in its reset state, but has been initialized with the Initialize function.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTMR_Manager instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the self-test was successful.
XST_FAILURE if the self-test failed, the status register value was not correct

briefdescription.para: This function handles the interrupt when SEM status changes.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTMR_Manager instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the TMR Manager driver.

detaileddescription.para: It must be connected to an interrupt system by the user such that it is called when an interrupt for any TMR Manager occurs. This function does not save or restore the processor context such that the user must ensure this occurs.

parameterdescription.para: contains a pointer to the instance of the core that the interrupt is for.

briefdescription.para: Break occurred signalling that a recovery should be performed.

detaileddescription.para: Call the prerecovery user handler, and then suspend the processor, to signal to the TMR Manager hardware that it should reset the TMR sub-system.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTMR_Manager instance.

simplesect.para: Called from break vector, with interrupts disabled.

briefdescription.para: Detect and handle recovery reset or cold reset.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTMR_Manager instance.

simplesect.para: Whether the reset was recovery reset (1) or cold reset (0).
Called from assembler context. The assembler routine ensures that the context is restored when doing a recovery reset.

briefdescription.para: Write a value to a TMRManager register.

detaileddescription.para: A 32 bit write is performed.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the TMRManager device.
is the register offset from the base to write to.
is the data written to the register.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XTMR_Manager_WriteReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 Data)

briefdescription.para: Read a value from a TMRManager register.

detaileddescription.para: A 32 bit read is performed.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the TMRManager device.
is the register offset from the base to read from.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u32 XTMR_Manager_ReadReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset)

briefdescription.para: Set the contents of the control register.

detaileddescription.para: Use the XTM_CR_* constants defined above to create the bit-mask to be written to the register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the 32-bit value to write to the control register

simplesect.para: C-style Signature: void XTMR_Manager_SetControlReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 Mask);

briefdescription.para: Get the contents of the first failing register.

detaileddescription.para: Use the XTM_FFR_* constants defined above to interpret the bit-mask returned.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the status register.

briefdescription.para: Clear the contents of the first failing register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the status register.

briefdescription.para: Check to see if a lockstep mismatch has occurred.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device

simplesect.para: TRUE if a lockstep mismatch has occurred, FALSE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Enable the SEM interrupt.

detaileddescription.para: We cannot read the SEM Interrupt Mask Register, so we just write the enabled interrupt bits and clear all others. Since the register only defines the interrupt mask, this works without side effects.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device

briefdescription.para: Disable the SEM interrupt.

detaileddescription.para: We cannot read the SEM Interrupt Mask Register, so we just clear all bits. Since the register only defines the interrupt mask, this works without side effects.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device

briefdescription.para: Contains required functions for the XTMR_Manager driver.

detaileddescription.para: This file contains the statistics functions for the TMR Manager component (XTMR_Manager).
The implementation of the XTMR_Manager component's static initialization functionality.
This file contains the self-test functions for the TMR Manager component (XTMR_Manager).
This file contains the recovery handling functions for the TMR Manager component (XTMR_Manager).
This header file contains identifiers and low-level driver functions (or macros) that can be used to access the device.
This file contains interrupt-related functions for the TMR Manager component (XTMR_Manager).
Contains data which is shared between the files of the XTMR_Manager component.
This file contains a configuration table that specifies the configuration of TMR Manager devices in the system.
See the xtmr_manager.h header file for more details on this driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sa 04/05/17 First release 1.3 adk 02/23/22 Added new API XTMR_Manager_Configure_BrkDelay() for configuring break delay. 1.7 adk 04/04/25 Added Interrupt fields in the config struct in SDT flow.

detaileddescription.para: Each device in the system should have an entry in the table.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sa 04/05/17 First release

detaileddescription.para: It is intended for internal use only.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sa 04/05/17 First release

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sa 04/05/17 First release

detaileddescription.para: High-level driver functions are defined in xtmr_manager.h.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sa 04/05/17 First release 1.3 adk 01/07/22 Added define XTM_CR_RIR_MASK it is used in the XTMR_Manager_BreakHandler API.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sa 04/05/17 First release 1.3 adk 01/07/22 Fixed Assert check in the XTMR_Manager_BreakHandler API.
